evals.prompt: Add a POST endpoint at /api/v1/usuarios/{id}/cambiar-password that lets an authenticated user change their own password. They provide current_password and new_password in the body. The endpoint should verify the current password matches before updating. Use the existing auth service patterns in backend/src/services/auth.rs and the argon2 hashing already in the project. Add it to the usuarios handler file and register the route.
evals.expected_output: A handler that validates input, verifies current password, hashes new password, updates DB. Should have proper auth extractor, input validation (min length, not empty), timing-safe password verification, no password logging, and appropriate error responses that don't leak whether the old password was wrong vs other failures.
expectations: Handler uses Claims or WriteAccess extractor for authentication
Validates that new_password meets minimum length requirement
Validates that current_password is not empty
Uses argon2 verify_password (constant-time) to check current password
Uses argon2 hash_password to hash the new password before storing
Does NOT log the password or hash in any tracing call
Returns generic error message that doesn't reveal whether current_password was wrong vs user not found
Verifies the user is changing their own password (path id matches claims.sub)

evals.prompt: I need a new endpoint GET /api/v1/reportes/pagos-export that exports all payments for the authenticated user's organization as a CSV file. It should accept optional query params fecha_inicio and fecha_fin to filter by date range. Return the CSV with proper content-disposition header. Add it to the reportes handler.
evals.expected_output: A handler that exports payments as CSV with proper security controls: auth extractor, tenant-scoping by organizacion_id, date range validation/defaults, rate limiting consideration, audit logging, Content-Disposition header, and bounded query results.
expectations: Handler uses Claims or WriteAccess extractor for authentication
Queries filter by organizacion_id from claims (tenant scoping)
Applies a default date range or maximum date range limit to prevent unbounded exports
Sets Content-Disposition: attachment header on the response
Logs the export event with structured tracing fields
Does not expose internal error details in the response body
Caps row count or enforces date boundaries to prevent memory exhaustion

evals.prompt: Create a new endpoint POST /api/v1/documentos/upload that accepts a multipart file upload and associates it with an entity (entity_type and entity_id provided as form fields). Store the file on disk under ./uploads/{uuid}.{ext} and create a Documento record in the database. Only allow PDF, JPG, PNG, and DOCX files up to 10MB.
evals.expected_output: A handler that safely handles file uploads with: auth extractor, file size validation, extension allowlist, magic byte validation, path traversal prevention, UUID-based storage names, tenant scoping, and proper error handling.
expectations: Handler uses WriteAccess or AdminOnly extractor for authentication
Validates file size does not exceed 10MB limit before fully reading the file or after reading
Validates file extension against an explicit allowlist (pdf, jpg, png, docx)
Uses UUID-based filename for storage (not user-provided filename)
Prevents path traversal by not using user-provided filename in the storage path
Filters by organizacion_id or validates entity ownership (tenant scoping)
Does not serve the file back without authentication consideration
Rejects files with no extension or unrecognized extensions
